verdict_reasoning: KRAS is a small GTPase with C-terminal lipidation (farnesylation and/or palmitoylation) that anchors it to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane and intracellular membranes (ER, Golgi, mitochondrial outer membrane). It lacks a transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or any mechanism to reach the outer face of the plasma membrane. The protein functions in the cytoplasm and at the inner PM face as a signaling node. Intracellular small-molecule inhibitors (e.g., KRAS G12C drugs) access it from inside the cell, not from the extracellular space.
